name: Slurm OOM Memory Triage
summary: Collect Slurm memory evidence for out-of-memory jobs.
description: This skill collects read-only Slurm accounting, job-state, optional efficiency, and log evidence for jobs that failed with out-of-memory or killed-process symptoms.

tools.name: sacct
tools.notes: Required for completed-job accounting evidence when Slurm accounting is available.

tools.name: scontrol
tools.notes: Optional job record evidence when the job is still visible to Slurm.

tools.name: seff
tools.notes: Optional efficiency summary when provided by the site.

inputs.name: job_id
inputs.description: Slurm job id, array task id, or job step id to inspect.

inputs.name: log_file
inputs.description: Optional Slurm stdout, stderr, or application log to scan for memory clues.

inputs.name: report_dir
inputs.description: Directory where the read-only evidence bundle should be written.

outputs.name: accounting_report
outputs.description: Slurm sacct table with job and step states, requested memory, maximum RSS, elapsed time, and allocated resources.

outputs.name: job_record
outputs.description: Optional scontrol job record for still-visible jobs.

outputs.name: log_memory_signals
outputs.description: Matching log lines for OOM, killed process, cgroup, allocator, data-loader, and memory-limit symptoms.

outputs.name: triage_summary
outputs.description: Human-readable prompts for deciding whether to increase memory, reduce concurrency, stage data differently, or gather stronger profiling evidence.

outputs.name: offline_evidence_review
outputs.description: Scheduler and application OOM signals extracted from saved public-safe fixtures.

examples.title: Review memory request choices after triage
Review saved OOM accounting and log evidence

tests.description: Validate the manifest and example files.
Check shell syntax for the evidence collection script.
Require matching scheduler and application OOM evidence in synthetic fixtures.
Run against a completed Slurm job with OUT_OF_MEMORY or killed-process symptoms and confirm the report stays read-only.
